name: markcss
description: Overrides the css of highlighted events. Make sure to use single quotes!

name: slider
description: Displays a slider widget to allow the user manipulate a value

name: s
description: Select a sound / sample by name. When using mininotation, you can also optionally supply 'n' and 'gain' parameters separated by ':'.

params.name: sound
params.description: The sound / pattern of sounds to pick

name: wt
description: Position in the wavetable of the wavetable oscillator

name: wtenv
description: Amount of envelope applied wavetable oscillator's position envelope

name: wtattack
description: Attack time of the wavetable oscillator's position envelope

name: wtdecay
description: Decay time of the wavetable oscillator's position envelope

name: wtsustain
description: Sustain time of the wavetable oscillator's position envelope

name: wtrelease
description: Release time of the wavetable oscillator's position envelope

name: wtrate
description: Rate of the LFO for the wavetable oscillator's position

name: wtsync
description: cycle synced rate of the LFO for the wavetable oscillator's position

name: wtdepth
description: Depth of the LFO for the wavetable oscillator's position

name: wtshape
description: Shape of the LFO for the wavetable oscillator's position

name: wtdc
description: DC offset of the LFO for the wavetable oscillator's position

name: wtskew
description: Skew of the LFO for the wavetable oscillator's position

params.name: skew
params.description: How much to bend the LFO shape

name: warp
description: Amount of warp (alteration of the waveform) to apply to the wavetable oscillator

name: warpattack
description: Attack time of the wavetable oscillator's warp envelope

name: warpdecay
description: Decay time of the wavetable oscillator's warp envelope

name: warpsustain
description: Sustain time of the wavetable oscillator's warp envelope

name: warprelease
description: Release time of the wavetable oscillator's warp envelope

name: warprate
description: Rate of the LFO for the wavetable oscillator's warp

name: warpdepth
description: Depth of the LFO for the wavetable oscillator's warp

name: warpshape
description: Shape of the LFO for the wavetable oscillator's warp

name: warpdc
description: DC offset of the LFO for the wavetable oscillator's warp

name: warpskew
description: Skew of the LFO for the wavetable oscillator's warp

params.name: skew
params.description: How much to bend the LFO shape

name: warpmode
description:
Type of warp (alteration of the waveform) to apply to the wavetable oscillator.

The current options are: none, asym, bendp, bendm, bendmp, sync, quant, fold, pwm, orbit, spin, chaos, primes, binary, brownian, reciprocal, wormhole, logistic, sigmoid, fractal, flip
examples:
s("morgana").bank("wt_digital").seg(8).note("F1").warp("0 0.25 0.5 0.75 1")
  .warpmode("<asym bendp spin logistic sync wormhole brownian>*2")

name: wtphaserand
description: Amount of randomness of the initial phase of the wavetable oscillator.

params.name: amount
params.description: Randomness of the initial phase. Between 0 (not random) and 1 (fully random)

name: warpenv
description: Amount of envelope applied wavetable oscillator's position envelope

name: warpsync
description: cycle synced rate of the LFO for the wavetable warp position

name: source
description: Define a custom webaudio node to use as a sound source.

name: n
description:
Selects the given index:

  • for samples, it picks the sample by index, with wrap around
  • for scales, it picks the scale degree
  • for voicings, it picks the voice index

name: i
description: Selects the given degree. Currently used in xen and tune:

name: note
description:
Plays the given note name or midi number. A note name consists of

  • a letter (a-g or A-G)
  • optional accidentals (b or #)
  • optional (possibly negative) octave number (0-9). Defaults to 3

Examples of valid note names: c, bb, Bb, f#, c3, A4, Eb2, c#5

You can also use midi numbers instead of note names, where 69 is mapped to A4 440Hz in 12EDO.

name: velocity
description: Sets the velocity from 0 to 1. Is multiplied together with gain.

name: gain
description: Controls the gain by an exponential amount.

name: postgain
description: Gain applied after all effects have been processed.

name: fmh
description:
Sets the Frequency Modulation Harmonicity Ratio. Controls the timbre of the sound. Whole numbers and simple ratios sound more natural, while decimal numbers and complex ratios sound metallic.

A number may be added afterwards to control the harmonicity of any of the 8 individual FMs (e.g. fmh2)
examples:
note("c e g b g e")
.fm(4)
.fmh("<1 2 1.5 1.61>")
._scope()

name: fmi
description:
Sets the Frequency Modulation of the synth. Controls the modulation index, which defines the brightness of the sound.

A number may be added afterwards to control the modulation index of any of the 8 individual FMs (e.g. fm3). Also, FMs may be routed into each other with matrix commands like fm13, which would send fm1 back into fm3
examples:
note("c e g b g e")
.fm("<0 1 2 8 32>")
._scope()

name: fmenv
description:
Ramp type of fm envelope. Exp might be a bit broken..

A number may be added afterwards to control the envelope of any of the 8 individual FMs (e.g. fmenv4)
examples:
note("c e g b g e")
.fm(4)
.fmdecay(.2)
.fmsustain(0)
.fmenv("<exp lin>")
._scope()

name: fmattack
description:
Attack time for the FM envelope: time it takes to reach maximum modulation

A number may be added afterwards to control the attack of the envelope of any of the 8 individual FMs (e.g. fmatt5)
examples:
note("c e g b g e")
.fm(4)
.fmattack("<0 .05 .1 .2>")
._scope()

name: fmwave
description:
Waveform of the fm modulator

A number may be added afterwards to control the waveform any of the 8 individual FMs (e.g. fmwave6)

name: fmdecay
description:
Decay time for the FM envelope: seconds until the sustain level is reached after the attack phase.

A number may be added afterwards to control the decay of the envelope of any of the 8 individual FMs (e.g. fmdec6)
examples:
note("c e g b g e")
.fm(4)
.fmdecay("<.01 .05 .1 .2>")
.fmsustain(.4)
._scope()

name: fmsustain
description:
Sustain level for the FM envelope: how much modulation is applied after the decay phase

A number may be added afterwards to control the sustain of the envelope of any of the 8 individual FMs (e.g. fmsus7)
examples:
note("c e g b g e")
.fm(4)
.fmdecay(.1)
.fmsustain("<1 .75 .5 0>")
._scope()

name: fmrelease
description:
Release time for the FM envelope: how much modulation is applied after the note is released

A number may be added afterwards to control the release of the envelope of any of the 8 individual FMs (e.g. fmrel8)

name: bank
description: Select the sound bank to use. To be used together with s. The bank name (+ "_") will be prepended to the value of s.
examples: s("bd sd [~ bd] sd").bank('RolandTR909') // = s("RolandTR909_bd RolandTR909_sd")

name: chorus
description: mix control for the chorus effect

name: attack
description: Amplitude envelope attack time: Specifies how long it takes for the sound to reach its peak value, relative to the onset.

name: decay
description: Amplitude envelope decay time: the time it takes after the attack time to reach the sustain level. Note that the decay is only audible if the sustain value is lower than 1.

name: sustain
description: Amplitude envelope sustain level: The level which is reached after attack / decay, being sustained until the offset.

name: release
description: Amplitude envelope release time: The time it takes after the offset to go from sustain level to zero.

name: bpf
description: Sets the center frequency of the band-pass filter. When using mininotation, you can also optionally supply the 'bpq' parameter separated by ':'.

name: bpq
description: Sets the band-pass q-factor (resonance).

name: begin
description: A pattern of numbers from 0 to 1. Skips the beginning of each sample, e.g. 0.25 to cut off the first quarter from each sample.

params.name: amount
params.description: between 0 and 1, where 1 is the length of the sample

name: end
description: The same as .begin, but cuts off the end off each sample.

params.name: length
params.description: 1 = whole sample, .5 = half sample, .25 = quarter sample etc..

name: loop
description: Loops the sample. Note that the tempo of the loop is not synced with the cycle tempo. To change the loop region, use loopBegin / loopEnd.

name: loopBegin
description: Begin to loop at a specific point in the sample (inbetween begin and end). Note that the loop point must be inbetween begin and end, and before loopEnd! Note: Samples starting with wt_ will automatically loop! (wt = wavetable)

params.name: time
params.description: between 0 and 1, where 1 is the length of the sample

name: loopEnd
description: End the looping section at a specific point in the sample (inbetween begin and end). Note that the loop point must be inbetween begin and end, and after loopBegin!

params.name: time
params.description: between 0 and 1, where 1 is the length of the sample

name: crush
description: Bit crusher effect.

params.name: depth
params.description: between 1 (for drastic reduction in bit-depth) to 16 (for barely no reduction).

name: coarse
description: Fake-resampling for lowering the sample rate. Caution: This effect seems to only work in chromium based browsers

params.name: factor
params.description: 1 for original 2 for half, 3 for a third and so on.

name: tremolo
description: Modulate the amplitude of a sound with a continuous waveform

name: tremolosync
description: Modulate the amplitude of a sound with a continuous waveform

name: tremolodepth
description: Depth of amplitude modulation

name: tremoloskew
description: Alter the shape of the modulation waveform

name: tremolophase
description: Alter the phase of the modulation waveform

params.name: offset
params.description: the offset in cycles of the modulation

name: tremoloshape
description: Shape of amplitude modulation
examples: note("{f g c d}%16").tremsync(4).tremoloshape("<sine tri square>").s("sawtooth")

name: drive
description: Filter overdrive for supported filter types
examples: note("{f g g c d a a#}%16".sub(17)).s("supersaw").lpenv(8).lpf(150).lpq(.8).ftype('ladder').drive("<.5 4>")

name: duckorbit
description:
Modulate the amplitude of an orbit to create a "sidechain" like effect.

Can be applied to multiple orbits with the ':' mininotation, e.g. duckorbit("2:3")

name: duckdepth
description:
The amount of ducking applied to target orbit

Can vary across orbits with the ':' mininotation, e.g. duckdepth("0.3:0.1"). Note: this requires first applying the effect to multiple orbits with e.g. duckorbit("2:3").

name: duckonset
description:
The time required for the ducked signal(s) to reach their lowest volume. Can be used to prevent clicking or for creative rhythmic effects.

Can vary across orbits with the ':' mininotation, e.g. duckonset("0:0.003"). Note: this requires first applying the effect to multiple orbits with e.g. duckorbit("2:3").
examples:
// Rhythmic
noise: s("pink").distort("2:1").orbit(4) // used rhythmically with 0.3 onset below
hhat: s("hh*16").orbit(7)
ducker: s("bd*4").bank("tr909").duckorbit("4:7").duckonset("0.3:0.003").duckattack(0.25)

name: duckattack
description:
The time required for the ducked signal(s) to return to their normal volume.

Can vary across orbits with the ':' mininotation, e.g. duckonset("0:0.003"). Note: this requires first applying the effect to multiple orbits with e.g. duckorbit("2:3").

name: byteBeatExpression
description: Create byte beats with custom expressions

name: byteBeatStartTime
description: Create byte beats with custom expressions

name: channels
description: Allows you to set the output channels on the interface

name: pw
description: Controls the pulsewidth of the pulse oscillator

name: pwrate
description: Controls the lfo rate for the pulsewidth of the pulse oscillator

name: pwsweep
description: Controls the lfo sweep for the pulsewidth of the pulse oscillator

name: phaser
description: Phaser audio effect that approximates popular guitar pedals.

name: phasersweep
description: The frequency sweep range of the lfo for the phaser effect. Defaults to 2000

name: phasercenter
description: The center frequency of the phaser in HZ. Defaults to 1000

name: phaserdepth
description: The amount the signal is affected by the phaser effect. Defaults to 0.75

name: channel
description: Choose the channel the pattern is sent to

name: cut
description: In the style of classic drum-machines, cut will stop a playing sample as soon as another samples with in same cutgroup is to be played. An example would be an open hi-hat followed by a closed one, essentially muting the open.

name: lpf
description:
Applies the cutoff frequency of the low-pass filter.

When using mininotation, you can also optionally add the 'lpq' parameter, separated by ':'.

name: lpenv
description: Sets the lowpass filter envelope modulation depth.

params.name: modulation
params.description: depth of the lowpass filter envelope between 0 and n

name: hpenv
description: Sets the highpass filter envelope modulation depth.

params.name: modulation
params.description: depth of the highpass filter envelope between 0 and n

name: bpenv
description: Sets the bandpass filter envelope modulation depth.

params.name: modulation
params.description: depth of the bandpass filter envelope between 0 and n

name: lpattack
description: Sets the attack duration for the lowpass filter envelope.

name: hpattack
description: Sets the attack duration for the highpass filter envelope.

name: bpattack
description: Sets the attack duration for the bandpass filter envelope.

name: lpdecay
description: Sets the decay duration for the lowpass filter envelope.

name: hpdecay
description: Sets the decay duration for the highpass filter envelope.

name: bpdecay
description: Sets the decay duration for the bandpass filter envelope.

name: lpsustain
description: Sets the sustain amplitude for the lowpass filter envelope.

name: hpsustain
description: Sets the sustain amplitude for the highpass filter envelope.

name: bpsustain
description: Sets the sustain amplitude for the bandpass filter envelope.

name: lprelease
description: Sets the release time for the lowpass filter envelope.

name: hprelease
description: Sets the release time for the highpass filter envelope.

name: bprelease
description: Sets the release time for the bandpass filter envelope.

name: ftype
description: Sets the filter type. The ladder filter is more aggressive. More types might be added in the future.
examples: note("{f g g c d a a#}%8").s("sawtooth").lpenv(4).lpf(500).ftype("<0 1 2>").lpq(1)

note("c f g g a c d4").fast(2)
.sound('sawtooth')
.lpf(200).fanchor(0)
.lpenv(3).lpq(1)
.ftype("<ladder 12db 24db>")

name: fanchor
description: controls the center of the filter envelope. 0 is unipolar positive, .5 is bipolar, 1 is unipolar negative
examples:
note("{f g g c d a a#}%8").s("sawtooth").lpf("{1000}%2")
.lpenv(8).fanchor("<0 .5 1>")

name: hpf
description:
Applies the cutoff frequency of the high-pass filter.

When using mininotation, you can also optionally add the 'hpq' parameter, separated by ':'.

name: lprate
description: Rate of the LFO for the lowpass filter

name: lpsync
description: Cycle-synced rate of the LFO for the lowpass filter

name: lpdepth
description: Depth of the LFO for the lowpass filter

name: lpdepthfrequency
description: Depth of the LFO for the lowpass filter, in HZ

name: lpshape
description: Shape of the LFO for the lowpass filter

name: lpdc
description: DC offset of the LFO for the lowpass filter

name: lpskew
description: Skew of the LFO for the lowpass filter

params.name: skew
params.description: How much to bend the LFO shape

name: bprate
description: Rate of the LFO for the bandpass filter

name: bpsync
description: Cycle-synced rate of the LFO for the bandpass filter

name: bpdepth
description: Depth of the LFO for the bandpass filter

name: bpdepthfrequency
description: Depth of the LFO for the bandpass filter, in HZ

name: bpshape
description: Shape of the LFO for the bandpass filter

name: bpdc
description: DC offset of the LFO for the bandpass filter

name: bpskew
description: Skew of the LFO for the bandpass filter

params.name: skew
params.description: How much to bend the LFO shape

name: hprate
description: Rate of the LFO for the highpass filter

name: hpsync
description: Cycle-synced rate of the LFO for the highpass filter

name: hpdepth
description: Depth of the LFO for the highpass filter

name: hpdepthfrequency
description: Depth of the LFO for the hipass filter, in hz

name: hpshape
description: Shape of the LFO for the highpass filter

name: hpdc
description: DC offset of the LFO for the highpass filter

name: hpskew
description: Skew of the LFO for the highpass filter

params.name: skew
params.description: How much to bend the LFO shape

name: vib
description: Applies a vibrato to the frequency of the oscillator.
examples:
// change the modulation depth with ":"
note("a e")
.vib("<.5 1 2 4 8 16>:12")
._scope()

name: noise
description: Adds pink noise to the mix

name: vibmod
description: Sets the vibrato depth in semitones. Only has an effect if vibrato | vib | v is is also set
examples:
// change the vibrato frequency with ":"
note("a e")
.vibmod("<.25 .5 1 2 12>:8")
._scope()

name: hpq
description: Controls the high-pass q-value.

name: lpq
description: Controls the low-pass q-value.

name: djf
description: DJ filter, below 0.5 is low pass filter, above is high pass filter.

params.name: cutoff
params.description: below 0.5 is low pass filter, above is high pass filter

name: delay
description:
Sets the level of the delay signal.

When using mininotation, you can also optionally add the 'delaytime' and 'delayfeedback' parameter, separated by ':'.

name: delayfeedback
description: Sets the level of the signal that is fed back into the delay. Caution: Values >= 1 will result in a signal that gets louder and louder! Don't do it

name: delayspeed
description: Sets the time of the delay effect.

params.name: delayspeed
params.description: controls the pitch of the delay feedback

name: delaytime
description: Sets the time of the delay effect in seconds.

name: delaysync
description: Sets the time of the delay effect in cycles.

name: detune
description: Set detune for stacked voices of supported oscillators.
examples: note("d f a a# a d3").fast(2).s("supersaw").detune("<.1 .2 .5 24.1>")

name: unison
description: Set number of stacked voices for supported oscillators.
examples: note("d f a a# a d3").fast(2).s("supersaw").unison("<1 2 7>")

name: spread
description: Set the stereo pan spread for supported oscillators
examples: note("d f a a# a d3").fast(2).s("supersaw").spread("<0 .3 1>")

name: fadeTime
description: Used when using begin/end or chop/striate and friends, to change the fade out time of the 'grain' envelope.

name: freq
description: Set frequency of sound.

params.name: frequency
params.description: in Hz. the audible range is between 20 and 20000 Hz

name: pattack
description: Attack time of pitch envelope.

name: pdecay
description: Decay time of pitch envelope.

name: prelease
description: Release time of pitch envelope
examples:
note("<c eb g bb> ~")
.release(.5) // to hear the pitch release
.prelease("<0 .1 .25 .5>")

name: penv
description: Amount of pitch envelope. Negative values will flip the envelope. If you don't set other pitch envelope controls, pattack:.2 will be the default.

name: pcurve
description: Curve of envelope. Defaults to linear. exponential is good for kicks

name: panchor
description:
Sets the range anchor of the envelope:

  • anchor 0: range = [note, note + penv]
  • anchor 1: range = [note - penv, note] If you don't set an anchor, the value will default to the psustain value.

name: label
description: Sets the displayed text for an event on the pianoroll

name: octave
description: Sets the default octave of a synth.

name: orbit
description: An orbit is a global parameter context for patterns. Patterns with the same orbit will share the same global effects.

name: bus
description:
A bus is a send which can be used for mixing patterns. It combines with.. s("bus") to play that bus through another pattern (for, say, applying non-linear effects like distortion to multiple signals)

otherPat.bmod(..) (to modulate another pattern with the bus)

name: busgain
description: Postgain multiplier prior to sending the signal to the audio bus.

name: pan
description: Sets position in stereo.

params.name: pan
params.description: between 0 and 1, from left to right (assuming stereo), once round a circle (assuming multichannel)

examples: s("bd rim sd rim bd ~ cp rim").pan(sine.slow(2))

name: panspan
description: Controls how much multichannel output is fanned out

params.name: span
params.description: between -inf and inf, negative is backwards ordering

name: pansplay
description: Controls how much multichannel output is spread

name: chord
description: The chord to voice

params.name: symbols
params.description: chord symbols to voice e.g., C, Eb, Fm7, G7. The symbols can be defined via addVoicings

examples: chord("<Am C D F Am E Am E>").voicing()

name: dictionary
description: Which dictionary to use for the voicings. This falls back to the default dictionary if not provided

params.name: dictionaryName
params.description: which dictionary (having been defined with addVoicings) to use

examples:
addVoicings('house', {
'': ['7 12 16', '0 7 16', '4 7 12'],
'm': ['0 3 7']
})
chord("<Am C D F Am E Am E>")
.dict('house').anchor(66)
.voicing().room(.5)

name: anchor
description: The top note to align the voicing to. Defaults to c5

params.name: anchorNote
params.description: the note to align the voicing or scale to

name: offset
description: Sets how the voicing is offset from the anchored position

params.name: shift
params.description: the amount to shift the voicing up or down

examples: chord("<Am C D F Am E Am E>").offset("<0 1 2 3 4 5>") // alter the voicing each time

name: octaves
description: How many octaves are voicing steps spread apart, defaults to 1
examples: chord("<Am C D F Am E Am E>").octaves("<2 4>").voicing()

name: mode
description: Remove anchor note from the voicing. Useful for melody harmonization

name: room
description:
Sets the level of reverb.

When using mininotation, you can also optionally add the 'size' parameter, separated by ':'.

name: roomlp
description: Reverb lowpass starting frequency (in hertz). When this property is changed, the reverb will be recaculated, so only change this sparsely..

name: roomdim
description: Reverb lowpass frequency at -60dB (in hertz). When this property is changed, the reverb will be recaculated, so only change this sparsely..

name: roomfade
description: Reverb fade time (in seconds). When this property is changed, the reverb will be recaculated, so only change this sparsely..

name: iresponse
description: Sets the sample to use as an impulse response for the reverb.

name: irspeed
description: Sets speed of the sample for the impulse response.

name: irbegin
description: Sets the beginning of the IR response sample

name: roomsize
description: Sets the room size of the reverb, see room. When this property is changed, the reverb will be recaculated, so only change this sparsely..

name: shape
description: (Deprecated) Wave shaping distortion. WARNING: can suddenly get unpredictably loud. Please use distort instead, which has a more predictable response curve second option in optional array syntax (ex: ".9:.5") applies a postgain to the output

name: distort
description: Wave shaping distortion. CAUTION: it can get loud. Second option in optional array syntax (ex: ".9:.5") applies a postgain to the output. Third option sets the waveshaping type. Most useful values are usually between 0 and 10 (depending on source gain). If you are feeling adventurous, you can turn it up to 11 and beyond ;)

name: distortvol
description: Postgain for waveshaping distortion.

name: distorttype
description: Type of waveshaping distortion to apply.
examples:
s("sine").note("F1*2").release(1)
  .penv(24).pdecay(0.05)
  .distort(rand.range(1, 8))
  .distorttype("<fold chebyshev scurve diode asym sinefold>")

name: compressor
description: Dynamics Compressor. The params are compressor("threshold:ratio:knee:attack:release") More info here

name: speed
description: Changes the speed of sample playback, i.e. a cheap way of changing pitch.

params.name: speed
params.description: inf to inf, negative numbers play the sample backwards.

name: stretch
description: Changes the pitch of the sample without changing its speed. The frequencies are multiplied by (factor + 1) for positive numbers and by max(factor / 4 + 1, 0) for negative numbers. So tuning up by octaves can be done with 1, 3, 7, ... and tuning down by octaves with -2, -3, -3.5...

params.name: factor
params.description: between -4 and inf. Positive increases pitch, 0 does nothing, negative decreases the pitch.

name: vowel
description: Formant filter to make things sound like vowels.

params.name: vowel
params.description: You can use a e i o u ae aa oe ue y uh un en an on, corresponding to [a] [e] [i] [o] [u] [æ] [ɑ] [ø] [y] [ɯ] [ʌ] [œ̃] [ɛ̃] [ɑ̃] [ɔ̃]. Aliases: aa = å = ɑ, oe = ø = ö, y = ı, ae = æ.

examples: s("bd sd mt ht bd [~ cp] ht lt").vowel("[a|e|i|o|u]")

name: density
description: crackle noise density

name: clip
description: Multiplies the duration with the given number. Also cuts samples off at the end if they exceed the duration.

name: duration
description: Sets the duration of the event in cycles. Similar to clip / legato, it also cuts samples off at the end if they exceed the duration.

name: color
description: Sets the color of the hap in visualizations like pianoroll or highlighting.

name: adsr
description: ADSR envelope: Combination of Attack, Decay, Sustain, and Release.

name: midichan
description: MIDI channel: Sets the MIDI channel for the event.

name: midiport
description: MIDI port: Sets the MIDI port for the event.

name: midicmd
description: MIDI command: Sends a MIDI command message.

name: control
description: MIDI control: Sends a MIDI control change message.

name: ccn
description: MIDI control number: Sends a MIDI control change message.

name: ccv
description: MIDI control value: Sends a MIDI control change message.

name: nrpnn
description: MIDI NRPN non-registered parameter number: Sends a MIDI NRPN non-registered parameter number message.

name: nrpv
description: MIDI NRPN non-registered parameter value: Sends a MIDI NRPN non-registered parameter value message.

name: progNum
description: MIDI program number: Sends a MIDI program change message.

name: sysex
description: MIDI sysex: Sends a MIDI sysex message.

name: sysexid
description: MIDI sysex ID: Sends a MIDI sysex identifier message.

name: sysexdata
description: MIDI sysex data: Sends a MIDI sysex message.

name: midibend
description: MIDI pitch bend: Sends a MIDI pitch bend message.

name: miditouch
description: MIDI key after touch: Sends a MIDI key after touch message.

name: oschost
description: The host to send open sound control messages to. Requires running the OSC bridge.

name: oscport
description: The port to send open sound control messages to. Requires running the OSC bridge.

name: as
description: Sets properties in a batch.

name: scrub
description: Allows you to scrub an audio file like a tape loop by passing values that represents the position in the audio file in the optional array syntax ex: "0.5:2", the second value controls the speed of playback

name: lfo
description:
Configures an LFO. Can be called in sequence like pat.lfo(...).lfo(...) to set up multiple LFOs. There are two ways to declare which control will be modulated:

  1. Explicitly put control in the config (e.g. lfo({ c: "lpf" }))
  2. If the control parameter is absent, the control immediately before the lfo call will be used (e.g. s("saw").lpf(500).lfo() to modulate lpf)

Modulators can be referred to by id so that they can be updated later e.g. inside a sometimes. See example below.

params.name: config.subControl
params.description: Sub-control name to append to the control key. Aliases: sc

params.name: config.dcoffset
params.description: DC offset / bias for the waveform. Aliases: dc

params.name: config.retrig
params.description: If > 0.5, the LFO will retrigger on each event. Aliases: rt

name: env
description:
Configures an envelope. Can be called in sequence like pat.env(...).env(...) to set up multiple envelopes There are two ways to declare which control will be modulated:

  1. Explicitly put control in the config (e.g. env({ c: "lpf" }))
  2. If the control parameter is absent, the control immediately before the env call will be used (e.g. s("saw").lpf(500).env({ a: 1 }) to modulate lpf)

Modulators can be referred to by id so that they can be updated later e.g. inside a sometimes. See example below.

params.name: config.subControl
params.description: Sub-control name to append to the control key. Aliases: sc

params.name: config.attack
params.description: Time to reach depth. Aliases: att, a

params.name: config.decay
params.description: Time to reach sustain. Aliases: dec, d

params.name: config.release
params.description: Time to return to nominal value. Aliases: rel, r

params.name: config.acurve
params.description: Snappiness of attack curve (-1 = relaxed, 1 = snappy). Aliases: ac

params.name: config.dcurve
params.description: Snappiness of decay curve (-1 = relaxed, 1 = snappy). Aliases: dc

params.name: config.rcurve
params.description: Snappiness of release curve (-1 = relaxed, 1 = snappy). Aliases: rc

examples:
s("pulse").lpf(500).env({ a: 1 })
  .env({ c: "s", a: 1 })
  .diode(0.3)
  .sometimes(x => x.env({ a: "0.5" }, 1)) // envelope #1 (0-indexed)

s("pulse").lpf(500).env({ a: 1 }, 'lpf_mod')
  .env({ c: "s", a: 1 })
  .diode(0.3)
  .sometimes(x => x.env({ a: "0.5" }, 'lpf_mod'))

name: bmod
description:
Modulates with the output from a given bus. Can be called in sequence like pat.bmod(...).bmod(...) to set up multiple modulators

Send to an audio bus with otherPat.bus(..).

There are two ways to declare which control will be modulated:

  1. Explicitly put control in the config (e.g. bmod({ id: 2, c: "lpf" }))
  2. If the control parameter is absent, the control immediately before the bmod call will be used (e.g. s("saw").lpf(500).bmod({ id: 2 }) to modulate lpf)

Modulators can be referred to by id so that they can be updated later e.g. inside a sometimes. See example below.

params.name: config.subControl
params.description: Sub-control name to append to the control key. Aliases: sc

name: transient
description: Transient shaper. Gives independent control over the emphasis on transients and sustains

name: drawLine
description:
Intended for a debugging, drawLine renders the pattern as a string, where each character represents the same time span. Should only be used with single characters as values, otherwise the character slots will be messed up. Character legend:

  • "|" cycle separator
  • "-" hold previous value
  • "." silence

name: euclid
description: Changes the structure of the pattern to form an Euclidean rhythm. Euclidean rhythms are rhythms obtained using the greatest common divisor of two numbers. They were described in 2004 by Godfried Toussaint, a Canadian computer scientist. Euclidean rhythms are really useful for computer/algorithmic music because they can describe a large number of rhythms with a couple of numbers.

name: euclidRot
description: Like euclid, but has an additional parameter for 'rotating' the resulting sequence.
examples:
// A Samba rhythm necklace from Brazil
note("c3").euclidRot(3,16,14)

name: euclidLegato
description: Similar to euclid, but each pulse is held until the next pulse, so there will be no gaps.

name: euclidLegatoRot
description: Similar to euclid, but each pulse is held until the next pulse, so there will be no gaps, and has an additional parameter for 'rotating' the resulting sequence

name: euclidish
description: A 'euclid' variant with an additional parameter that morphs the resulting rhythm from 0 (no morphing) to 1 (completely 'even'). For example sound("bd").euclidish(3,8,0) would be the same as sound("bd").euclid(3,8), and sound("bd").euclidish(3,8,1) would be the same as sound("bd bd bd"). sound("bd").euclidish(3,8,0.5) would have a groove somewhere between. Inspired by the work of Malcom Braff.

params.name: groove
params.description: exists between the extremes of 0 (straight euclidian) and 1 (straight pulse)

name: clearScope
description: Clears all user-defined variables and functions from the scope. This removes variables created during block-based evaluation.

name: withValue
description: Returns a new pattern, with the function applied to the value of each hap. It has the alias fmap.

name: filterHaps
description: Returns a new Pattern, which only returns haps that meet the given test.

params.name: hap_test
params.description: a function which returns false for haps to be removed from the pattern

name: filterValues
description: As with filterHaps, but the function is applied to values inside haps.
examples:
const drums = s("bd sd bd sd")
kick: drums.filterValues((v) => v.s === 'bd').duck(2)
snare: drums.filterValues((v) => v.s === 'sd')
bass: s("saw!4").note("G#1").lpf(80).lpenv(4).orbit(2)

name: asNumber
description: Returns a new pattern with all values parsed as numerals.

name: layer
description: Layers the result of the given function(s). Like superimpose, but without the original pattern:

name: superimpose
description: Superimposes the result of the given function(s) on top of the original pattern:

name: log
description: Writes the content of the current event to the console (visible in the side menu).

name: logValues
description: A simplified version of log which writes all "values" (various configurable parameters) within the event to the console (visible in the side menu).

name: into
description: Breaks a pattern into pieces according to the structure of a given pattern. True values in the given pattern cause the corresponding subcycle of the source pattern to be looped, and for an (optional) given function to be applied. False values result in the corresponding part of the source pattern to be played unchanged.

name: arpWith
description: Selects indices in in stacked notes.

name: arp
description: Selects indices in in stacked notes.

name: set
description:
When called on a pattern a, with a input pattern b (a.set(b)), combines a and b such that anything defined in b and anything defined in a that is not defined in b will be in the resulting pattern.

The structure is maintained from a, because the default pattern alignment is in, see the section on Pattern Alignment in the technical manual in the docs

This is the inverse of keep

See examples below

name: keep
description:
When called on a pattern a, with a input pattern b (a.keep(b)), combines a and b such that anything defined in a, and anything defined in b that is not defined in a will be in the resulting pattern

The structure is maintained from a, because the default pattern alignment is in, see the section on Pattern Alignment in the technical manual in the docs

This is the inverse of set

See examples below

name: add
description: Assumes a pattern of numbers. Adds the given number to each item in the pattern.

name: sub
description: Like add, but the given numbers are subtracted.
examples:
n("0 2 4".sub("<0 1 2 3>")).scale("C4:minor")
// See add for more information.

name: mul
description: Multiplies each number by the given factor.

name: div
description: Divides each number by the given factor.

name: struct
description: Applies the given structure to the pattern:
examples:
note("c,eb,g")
  .struct("x ~ x ~ ~ x ~ x ~ ~ ~ x ~ x ~ ~")
  .slow(2)

name: mask
description: Returns silence when mask is 0 or "~"

name: reset
description: Resets the pattern to the start of the cycle for each onset of the reset pattern.

name: restart
description: Restarts the pattern for each onset of the restart pattern. While reset will only reset the current cycle, restart will start from cycle 0.

name: setDefaultJoin
description:
Sets the default method of combining events from two patterns (aka alignment) in Strudel. The default method is 'in', meaning that patterns to the left will (typically) dictate the event timings when combined with patterns to the right. By changing alignment to 'out', the opposite will happen. With 'mix', they will combine their event timings.

Note that we say the default method, because alignments can also be set explicitly with calls like 'add.mix', 'set.squeeze', etc.
examples:
setDefaultJoin('mix') // also try 'in', 'out', 'squeeze', etc.
s("saw").vel("1 0.5").note("F A C E").delay("0 0.2 0.3")

name: gap
description: Does absolutely nothing, but with a given metrical 'steps'

name: silence
description: Does absolutely nothing..

name: sequenceP
description: Takes a list of patterns, and returns a pattern of lists.

name: stack
description: The given items are played at the same time at the same length.

name: slowcat
description: Concatenation: combines a list of patterns, switching between them successively, one per cycle.

name: slowcatPrime
description: Concatenation: combines a list of patterns, switching between them successively, one per cycle. Unlike slowcat, this version will skip cycles.

name: cat
description: The given items are concatenated, where each one takes one cycle.

name: arrange
description: Allows to arrange multiple patterns together over multiple cycles. Takes a variable number of arrays with two elements specifying the number of cycles and the pattern to use.

name: seqPLoop
description: Similarly to arrange, allows you to arrange multiple patterns together over multiple cycles. Unlike arrange, you specify a start and stop time for each pattern rather than duration, which means that patterns can overlap.

name: sequence
description: See fastcat

name: seq
description: Like cat, but the items are crammed into one cycle.

name: register
description: Registers a new pattern method. The method is added to the Pattern class + the standalone function is returned from register.

params.name: name
params.description: name of the function, or an array of names to be used as synonyms

params.name: func
params.description: function with 1 or more params, where last is the current pattern

params.name: patternify
params.description: defaults to true; if set to false, you will have more control over the arguments to func as they will be in their raw form and it will be up to you to patternify them and/or query them for values

name: round
description: Assumes a numerical pattern. Returns a new pattern with all values rounded to the nearest integer.

name: floor
description: Assumes a numerical pattern. Returns a new pattern with all values set to their mathematical floor. E.g. 3.7 replaced with to 3, and -4.2 replaced with -5.

name: ceil
description: Assumes a numerical pattern. Returns a new pattern with all values set to their mathematical ceiling. E.g. 3.2 replaced with 4, and -4.2 replaced with -4.

name: range
description: Assumes a numerical pattern, containing unipolar values in the range 0 .. 1. Returns a new pattern with values scaled to the given min/max range. Most useful in combination with continuous patterns.

name: rangex
description: Assumes a numerical pattern, containing unipolar values in the range 0 .. 1 Returns a new pattern with values scaled to the given min/max range, following an exponential curve.

name: range2
description: Assumes a numerical pattern, containing bipolar values in the range -1 .. 1 Returns a new pattern with values scaled to the given min/max range.

name: ratio
description: Allows dividing numbers via list notation using ":". Returns a new pattern with just numbers.

name: compress
description: Compress each cycle into the given timespan, leaving a gap
examples:
cat(
  s("bd sd").compress(.25,.75),
  s("~ bd sd ~")
)

name: fastGap
description: speeds up a pattern like fast, but rather than it playing multiple times as fast would it instead leaves a gap in the remaining space of the cycle. For example, the following will play the sound pattern "bd sn" only once but compressed into the first half of the cycle, i.e. twice as fast.

name: focus
description: Similar to compress, but doesn't leave gaps, and the 'focus' can be bigger than a cycle

name: ply
description: The ply function repeats each event the given number of times.

name: fast
description: Speed up a pattern by the given factor. Used by "*" in mini notation.
examples: s("bd hh sd hh").fast(2) // s("[bd hh sd hh]*2")

name: hurry
description: Both speeds up the pattern (like 'fast') and the sample playback (like 'speed').

name: slow
description: Slow down a pattern over the given number of cycles. Like the "/" operator in mini notation.
examples: s("bd hh sd hh").slow(2) // s("[bd hh sd hh]/2")

name: inside
description: Carries out an operation 'inside' a cycle.

name: outside
description: Carries out an operation 'outside' a cycle.

name: lastOf
description: Applies the given function every n cycles, starting from the last cycle.

name: firstOf
description: Applies the given function every n cycles, starting from the first cycle.

name: every
description: An alias for firstOf

name: apply
description: Applies the given function to the pattern. Like layer, but with a single function:

name: cpm
description: Plays the pattern at the given cycles per minute.

name: early
description: Nudge a pattern to start earlier in time. Equivalent of Tidal's <~ operator

name: late
description: Nudge a pattern to start later in time. Equivalent of Tidal's ~> operator

name: zoom
description: Plays a portion of a pattern, specified by the beginning and end of a time span. The new resulting pattern is played over the time period of the original pattern:
examples:
s("bd*2 hh*3 [sd bd]*2 perc").zoom(0.25, 0.75)
// s("hh*3 [sd bd]*2") // equivalent

name: bite
description: Splits a pattern into the given number of slices, and plays them according to a pattern of slice numbers. Similar to slice, but slices up patterns rather than sound samples.

name: linger
description: Selects the given fraction of the pattern and repeats that part to fill the remainder of the cycle.

name: segment
description: Samples the pattern at a rate of n events per cycle. Useful for turning a continuous pattern into a discrete one.

name: swingBy
description: The function swingBy x n breaks each cycle into n slices, and then delays events in the second half of each slice by the amount x, which is relative to the size of the (half) slice. So if x is 0 it does nothing, 0.5 delays for half the note duration, and 1 will wrap around to doing nothing again. The end result is a shuffle or swing-like rhythm

name: swing
description: Shorthand for swingBy with 1/3:

name: invert
description: Swaps 1s and 0s in a binary pattern.

name: when
description: Applies the given function whenever the given pattern is in a true state.

name: off
description: Superimposes the function result on top of the original pattern, delayed by the given time.

name: brak
description: Returns a new pattern where every other cycle is played once, twice as fast, and offset in time by one quarter of a cycle. Creates a kind of breakbeat feel.

name: rev
description: Reverse all cycles in a pattern. See also revv for reversing a whole pattern.

name: revv
description: Reverse a whole pattern. See also rev for reversing each cycle.

name: pressBy
description: Like press, but allows you to specify the amount by which each event is shifted. pressBy(0.5) is the same as press, while pressBy(1/3) shifts each event by a third of its timespan.

name: press
description: Syncopates a rhythm, by shifting each event halfway into its timespan.

name: hush
description: Silences a pattern.

name: palindrome
description: Applies rev to a pattern every other cycle, so that the pattern alternates between forwards and backwards.

name: juxBy
description: Jux with adjustable stereo width. 0 = mono, 1 = full stereo.
examples: s("bd lt [~ ht] mt cp ~ bd hh").juxBy("<0 .5 1>/2", rev)

name: juxFlipBy
description: Like juxBy, except it flips the ears each cycle.
examples: s("bd lt [~ ht] mt cp ~ bd hh").juxFlipBy(".8", rev)

name: jux
description: The jux function creates strange stereo effects, by applying a function to a pattern, but only in the right-hand channel.
examples: s("bd lt [~ ht] mt cp ~ bd hh").jux(rev)
s("bd lt [~ ht] mt cp ~ bd hh").jux(press)
s("bd lt [~ ht] mt cp ~ bd hh").jux(iter(4))

name: juxFlip
description: Like jux, but flips the ears each cycle.
examples: s("bd lt [~ ht] mt cp ~ bd hh").juxFlip(rev)
s("bd lt [~ ht] mt cp ~ bd hh").juxFlip(press)
s("bd lt [~ ht] mt cp ~ bd hh").juxFlip(iter(4))

name: echoWith
description: Superimpose and offset multiple times, applying the given function each time.

params.name: func
params.description: function to apply, given the pattern and the iteration index

name: echo
description: Superimpose and offset multiple times, gradually decreasing the velocity

name: stut
description: Deprecated. Like echo, but the last 2 parameters are flipped.

name: plyWith
description: The plyWith function repeats each event the given number of times, applying the given function to each event.\n

name: plyForEach
description: The plyForEach function repeats each event the given number of times, applying the given function to each event. This version of ply uses the iteration index as an argument to the function, similar to echoWith.

params.name: func
params.description: function to apply, given the pattern and the iteration index

name: iter
description: Divides a pattern into a given number of subdivisions, plays the subdivisions in order, but increments the starting subdivision each cycle. The pattern wraps to the first subdivision after the last subdivision is played.

name: iterBack
description: Like iter, but plays the subdivisions in reverse order. Known as iter' in tidalcycles

name: repeatCycles
description: Repeats each cycle the given number of times.

name: chunk
description: Divides a pattern into a given number of parts, then cycles through those parts in turn, applying the given function to each part in turn (one part per cycle).

name: chunkBack
description: Like chunk, but cycles through the parts in reverse order. Known as chunk' in tidalcycles

name: fastChunk
description: Like chunk, but the cycles of the source pattern aren't repeated for each set of chunks.

name: chunkInto
description: Like chunk, but the function is applied to a looped subcycle of the source pattern.
examples:
sound("bd sd ht lt bd - cp lt").chunkInto(4, hurry(2))
  .bank("tr909")

name: chunkBackInto
description: Like chunkInto, but moves backwards through the chunks.
examples:
sound("bd sd ht lt bd - cp lt").chunkInto(4, hurry(2))
  .bank("tr909")

name: ribbon
description: Loops the pattern inside an offset for cycles. If you think of the entire span of time in cycles as a ribbon, you can cut a single piece and loop it.

name: tag
description: Tags each Hap with an identifier. Good for filtering. The function populates Hap.context.tags (Array).
examples:
s("saw!16").note("F1")
  .lpf(tri.range(40, 80).slow(4)).lpenv(5).lpq(4).lpd(0.15)
  .when(rand.late(0.1).gte(0.5), x => x.transpose("12").tag('altered'))
  .when(rand.late(0.2).gte(0.5), x => x.s("square").tag('altered'))
  .when("<0 1>", x => x.filter((hap) => hap.hasTag('altered')))

name: filter
description: Filters haps using the given function

name: filterWhen
description: Filters haps by their begin time

name: within
description: Use within to apply a function to only a part of a pattern.

params.name: func
params.description: function to be applied to the sub-pattern

name: pace
description:
Experimental

Speeds a pattern up or down, to fit to the given number of steps per cycle.
examples:
sound("bd sd cp").pace(4)
// The same as sound("{bd sd cp}%4") or sound("<bd sd cp>*4")

name: polymeter
description:
Experimental

Aligns the steps of the patterns, creating polymeters. The patterns are repeated until they all fit the cycle. For example, in the below the first pattern is repeated twice, and the second is repeated three times, to fit the lowest common multiple of six steps.
examples:
// The same as note("{c eb g, c2 g2}%6")
polymeter("c eb g", "c2 g2").note()

name: stepcat
description: 'Concatenates' patterns like fastcat, but proportional to a number of steps per cycle. The steps can either be inferred from the pattern, or provided as a [length, pattern] pair. Has the alias timecat.
examples:
stepcat("bd sd cp","hh hh").sound()
// the same as "bd sd cp hh hh".sound()

name: stepalt
description:
Experimental

Concatenates patterns stepwise, according to an inferred 'steps per cycle'. Similar to stepcat, but if an argument is a list, the whole pattern will alternate between the elements in the list.
examples:
stepalt(["bd cp", "mt"], "bd").sound()
// The same as "bd cp bd mt bd".sound()

name: take
description:
Experimental

Takes the given number of steps from a pattern (dropping the rest). A positive number will take steps from the start of a pattern, and a negative number from the end.
examples:
"bd cp ht mt".take("2").sound()
// The same as "bd cp".sound()

"bd cp ht mt".take("1 2 3").sound()
// The same as "bd bd cp bd cp ht".sound()

"bd cp ht mt".take("-1 -2 -3").sound()
// The same as "mt ht mt cp ht mt".sound()

name: drop
description:
Experimental

Drops the given number of steps from a pattern. A positive number will drop steps from the start of a pattern, and a negative number from the end.

name: extend
description:
Experimental

extend is similar to fast in that it increases its density, but it also increases the step count accordingly. So stepcat("a b".extend(2), "c d") would be the same as "a b a b c d", whereas stepcat("a b".fast(2), "c d") would be the same as "[a b] [a b] c d".
examples:
stepcat(
  sound("bd bd - cp").extend(2),
  sound("bd - sd -")
).pace(8)

name: replicate
description:
Experimental

replicate is similar to fast in that it increases its density, but it also increases the step count accordingly. So stepcat("a b".replicate(2), "c d") would be the same as "a b a b c d", whereas stepcat("a b".fast(2), "c d") would be the same as "[a b] [a b] c d".

TODO: find out how this function differs from extend
examples:
stepcat(
  sound("bd bd - cp").replicate(2),
  sound("bd - sd -")
).pace(8)

name: expand
description:
Experimental

Expands the step size of the pattern by the given factor.

name: contract
description:
Experimental

Contracts the step size of the pattern by the given factor. See also expand.

name: shrink
description:
Experimental

Progressively shrinks the pattern by 'n' steps until there's nothing left, or if a second value is given (using mininotation list syntax with :), that number of times. A positive number will progressively drop steps from the start of a pattern, and a negative number from the end.

name: grow
description:
Experimental

Progressively grows the pattern by 'n' steps until the full pattern is played, or if a second value is given (using mininotation list syntax with :), that number of times. A positive number will progressively grow steps from the start of a pattern, and a negative number from the end.

name: tour
description:
Experimental

Inserts a pattern into a list of patterns. On the first repetition it will be inserted at the end of the list, then moved backwards through the list on successive repetitions. The patterns are added together stepwise, with all repetitions taking place over a single cycle. Using pace to set the number of steps per cycle is therefore usually recommended.
examples:
"[c g]".tour("e f", "e f g", "g f e c").note()
   .sound("folkharp")
   .pace(8)

name: zip
description:
Experimental

'zips' together the steps of the provided patterns. This can create a long repetition, taking place over a single, dense cycle. Using pace to set the number of steps per cycle is therefore usually recommended.
examples:
zip("e f", "e f g", "g [f e] a f4 c").note()
   .sound("folkharp")
   .pace(8)

name: timecat
description: Aliases for stepcat

name: chop
description: Cuts each sample into the given number of parts, allowing you to explore a technique known as 'granular synthesis'. It turns a pattern of samples into a pattern of parts of samples.
examples:
samples({ rhodes: 'https://cdn.freesound.org/previews/132/132051_316502-lq.mp3' })
s("rhodes")
 .chop(4)
 .rev() // reverse order of chops
 .loopAt(2) // fit sample into 2 cycles

name: striate
description: Cuts each sample into the given number of parts, triggering progressive portions of each sample at each loop.

name: loopAt
description: Makes the sample fit the given number of cycles by changing the speed.

name: slice
description: Chops samples into the given number of slices, triggering those slices with a given pattern of slice numbers. Instead of a number, it also accepts a list of numbers from 0 to 1 to slice at specific points.

name: onTriggerTime
description: make something happen on event time uses browser timeout which is innacurate for audio tasks

name: splice
description: Works the same as slice, but changes the playback speed of each slice to match the duration of its step.

name: fit
description: Makes the sample fit its event duration. Good for rhythmical loops like drum breaks. Similar to loopAt.

name: loopAtCps
description: Makes the sample fit the given number of cycles and cps value, by changing the speed. deprecated: use loopAt or fit instead, together with setCps / setCpm.

name: ref
description: exposes a custom value at query time. basically allows mutating state without evaluation

name: xfade
description:
Cross-fades between left and right from 0 to 1:

  • 0 = (full left, no right)
  • .5 = (both equal)
  • 1 = (no left, full right)

name: beat
description: creates a structure pattern from divisions of a cycle especially useful for creating rhythms

name: morph
description: Takes two binary rhythms represented as lists of 1s and 0s, and a number between 0 and 1 that morphs between them. The two lists should contain the same number of true values.

name: soft
description: Soft-clipping distortion

name: hard
description: Hard-clipping distortion

name: cubic
description: Cubic polynomial distortion

name: diode
description: Diode-emulating distortion

name: asym
description: Asymmetrical diode distortion

name: fold
description: Wavefolding distortion

name: sinefold
description: Wavefolding distortion composed with sinusoid

name: chebyshev
description: Distortion via Chebyshev polynomials

name: parray
description: Turns a list of patterns into a single pattern which outputs list-values

name: partials
description:
Scale the magnitude of the harmonics of one of the core synths ('sine', 'tri', 'saw', ..)

Can also be used to create a new synth via s('user').partials(...)

params.name: magnitudes
params.description: List of [0, 1] magnitudes for partials. 0th entry is the fundamental harmonic (i.e. DC offset is skipped)

name: phases
description: Rotates the harmonics of one of the core synths ('sine', 'tri', 'saw', 'user', ..) by a list of phases

params.name: phases
params.description: List of [0, 1) phases for partials. 0th entry is the fundamental phase (i.e. DC offset is skipped)

name: FX
description: Establishes an FX chain. Can be called by chaining .FX(fx1).FX(fx2).. calls and/or in a single .FX(fx1, fx2, ..) call. The fx1, .. are patterns which establish the controls of the given effect. See examples.
examples:
$: s("[sbd <hh [bd | lt | oh]>]*4").dec(.4)
  .FX(
    phaser(0.5).gain(2),
    bpf(800),
    distort(1.3),
    room(0.2),
    delay(0.5).gain(1.25),
    distort(0.3),
  ).fxr(1.7) // sets release time of effects (like delay)

$: s("saw").fm(0.5)
  .delay(0.3) // outer effects are applied *last*
  .FX(coarse(4)) // first coarse
  .FX(lpf(500).lpe(4).lpa(1).lpd(2)) // then lpf
  .FX(distort(1)) // then distort

name: K
description:
Produces a Kabelsalat modular sound engine. This can be used as either an effect (by including audioin() at the beginning of your kabel expression) or as a sound source (via any expression which doesn't start with audioin()).

Some helpers you have available to you:

  • Strudel mini notation works fine in K(..) via "" or ``
  • More complex Strudel expressions (like "0 1 2".fast(4) or irand(24)) can be written by wrapping them in S(..) inside your Kabel code
  • We expose Strudel's note frequency under sFreq and Strudel's gate information under sGate
  • You can use more complex multi-line expressions (like let x = a; let y = b; x.lpf(y);) by wrapping them inside a function in K (see example).
examples:
note("A c e".fast(4)).transpose("<0 2 4 6 8>")
  .scale("F:minor").transpose("12")
  .s("saw")
  .K(
    // audioin().mul(sGate.adsr(0.001, 0.3, 0, 0.2)) // as effect
    saw(saw(sFreq / "2!3 16").mul(8).add(sFreq).lag("0!3 0.1")).mul(0.3) // as source
    .mul(sGate.adsr(0, 0.15, 0.5, "0.1!3 1"))
    .lpf(sGate.adsr(0, 0.2, 0.3, 0.2).mul(1).add(0))
    .add(x => x.delay(S("0.3 0.2".fast(2))).mul(0.7))
    .add(x => x.delay("0.03 [0.08 0.01] 0.01 0.013").mul(0.77)).mul(0.7)
    .add(x => x.delay(.13).mul(0.7))
    .out()
  )

name: base
description: Creates a pattern of numbers in base b from a number or pattern of numbers limited to d digits long from the right

params.name: n
params.description: number to convert (can be a pattern or array)

params.name: b
params.description: base to convert to (defaults to 10) (can be a pattern)

params.name: d
params.description: max number of digits to produce for each n (defaults to 0 for all) (can be a pattern)

name: pick
description: Picks patterns (or plain values) either from a list (by index) or a lookup table (by name). Similar to inhabit, but maintains the structure of the original patterns.
examples: note("<0 1 2!2 3>".pick(["g a", "e f", "f g f g" , "g c d"]))

name: pickmod
description: The same as pick, but if you pick a number greater than the size of the list, it wraps around, rather than sticking at the maximum value. For example, if you pick the fifth pattern of a list of three, you'll get the second one.

name: pickF
description: pickF lets you use a pattern of numbers to pick which function to apply to another pattern.

params.name: lookup
params.description: the array or lookup object of functions from which to pull

examples:
note("<c2 d2>(3,8)").s("square")
.pickF("<jr l> f", { jr:jux(rev), f:fast(2), l:x=>x.lpf(800) })

name: pickmodF
description: The same as pickF, but if you pick a number greater than the size of the functions list, it wraps around, rather than sticking at the maximum value.

params.name: lookup
params.description: the array or lookup object of functions from which to pull

name: pickOut
description: Similar to pick, but it applies an outerJoin instead of an innerJoin.

name: pickmodOut
description: The same as pickOut, but if you pick a number greater than the size of the list, it wraps around, rather than sticking at the maximum value.

name: pickRestart
description: Similar to pick, but the choosen pattern is restarted when its index is triggered.

name: pickmodRestart
description: The same as pickRestart, but if you pick a number greater than the size of the list, it wraps around, rather than sticking at the maximum value.

name: pickReset
description: Similar to pick, but the choosen pattern is reset when its index is triggered.

name: pickmodReset
description: The same as pickReset, but if you pick a number greater than the size of the list, it wraps around, rather than sticking at the maximum value.

name: inhabit
description: Picks patterns (or plain values) either from a list (by index) or a lookup table (by name). Similar to pick, but cycles are squeezed into the target ('inhabited') pattern.

name: inhabitmod
description: The same as inhabit, but if you pick a number greater than the size of the list, it wraps around, rather than sticking at the maximum value. For example, if you pick the fifth pattern of a list of three, you'll get the second one.

name: squeeze
description: Pick from the list of values (or patterns of values) via the index using the given pattern of integers. The selected pattern will be compressed to fit the duration of the selecting event
examples: note(squeeze("<0@2 [1!2] 2>", ["g a", "f g f g" , "g a c d"]))

name: setcpm
description: Changes the global tempo to the given cycles per minute

name: all
description:
Applies a function to all the running patterns. Note that the patterns are grouped together into a single stack before the function is applied. This is probably what you want, but see each for a version that applies the function to each pattern separately.

Note: Patterns must be labeled (e.g. with $:) to be picked up by all. An unlabeled pattern such as note("c4") is not registered and will produce no audio when all is present. Use $: note("c4") instead.

$: sound("bd - cp sd")
$: sound("hh*8")
all(fast("<2 3>"))

$: sound("bd - cp sd")
$: sound("hh*8")
all(x => x.pianoroll())

name: each
description:
Applies a function to each of the running patterns separately. This is intended for future use with upcoming 'stepwise' features. See all for a version that applies the function to all the patterns stacked together into a single pattern.

Note: Patterns must be labeled (e.g. with $:) to be picked up by each. An unlabeled pattern such as note("c4") is not registered and will produce no audio when each is present. Use $: note("c4") instead.

$: sound("bd - cp sd")
$: sound("hh*8")
each(fast("<2 3>"))

name: saw
description: A sawtooth signal between 0 and 1.

name: saw2
description: A sawtooth signal between -1 and 1 (like saw, but bipolar).

name: isaw
description: A sawtooth signal between 1 and 0 (like saw, but flipped).

name: isaw2
description: A sawtooth signal between 1 and -1 (like saw2, but flipped).

name: sine2
description: A sine signal between -1 and 1 (like sine, but bipolar).

name: sine
description: A sine signal between 0 and 1.

name: cosine
description: A cosine signal between 0 and 1.

name: cosine2
description: A cosine signal between -1 and 1 (like cosine, but bipolar).

name: square
description: A square signal between 0 and 1.

name: square2
description: A square signal between -1 and 1 (like square, but bipolar).

name: tri
description: A triangle signal between 0 and 1.

name: tri2
description: A triangle signal between -1 and 1 (like tri, but bipolar).

name: itri
description: An inverted triangle signal between 1 and 0 (like tri, but flipped).

name: itri2
description: An inverted triangle signal between -1 and 1 (like itri, but bipolar).

name: time
description: A signal representing the cycle time.

name: mousex
description: The mouse's x position value ranges from 0 to 1.

name: mousey
description: The mouse's y position value ranges from 0 to 1.

name: useRNG
description: Sets which random number generator to use. Historically Strudel would use useRNG('legacy'), which remains the default. To use a new more statistically precise RNG, try useRNG('precise').

name: run
description: A discrete pattern of numbers from 0 to n-1

name: binary
description: Creates a binary pattern from a number.

name: binaryN
description: Creates a binary pattern from a number, padded to n bits long.

name: binaryL
description: Creates a binary list pattern from a number.

params.name: n
params.description: input number to convert to binary s("saw").seg(8) .partials(binaryL(irand(4096).add(1)))

name: binaryNL
description: Creates a binary list pattern from a number, padded to n bits long.

name: randL
description: Creates a list of random numbers of the given length

name: shuffle
description: Slices a pattern into the given number of parts, then plays those parts in random order. Each part will be played exactly once per cycle.

name: scramble
description: Slices a pattern into the given number of parts, then plays those parts at random. Similar to shuffle, but parts might be played more than once, or not at all, per cycle.

name: withSeed
description: Modify a pattern by applying a function to the randomSeed control if present

params.name: func
params.description: Function from seed (or undefined) to seed (or undefined)

name: seed
description: Change the seed for random signals. Normally, random signals depend on time, so two patterns at the same time will have the same random values. Specifying a new seed changes the signal output by rand. This also affects other functions that use randomness, like shuffle and sometimes.

params.name: n
params.description: A new seed. Can be any number.

examples:
$: s("hh*4").degrade();
$: s("bd*4").degrade().seed(1); // Will degrade different events from the hi-hat

name: rand
description: A continuous pattern of random numbers, between 0 and 1.

name: rand2
description: A continuous pattern of random numbers, between -1 and 1

name: brandBy
description: A continuous pattern of 0 or 1 (binary random), with a probability for the value being 1

name: brand
description: A continuous pattern of 0 or 1 (binary random)

name: irand
description: A continuous pattern of random integers, between 0 and n-1.
examples:
// randomly select scale notes from 0 - 7 (= C to C)
n(irand(8)).struct("x x*2 x x*3").scale("C:minor")

name: chooseWith
description: Choose from the list of values (or patterns of values) using the given pattern of numbers, which should be in the range of 0..1

name: chooseInWith
description: As with {chooseWith}, but the structure comes from the chosen values, rather than the pattern you're using to choose with.

name: choose
description: Chooses randomly from the given list of elements.

name: choose2
description: As with choose, but the pattern that this method is called on should be in the range -1 .. 1

name: chooseCycles
description: Picks one of the elements at random each cycle.

name: wchoose
description: Chooses randomly from the given list of elements by giving a probability to each element

name: wchooseCycles
description: Picks one of the elements at random each cycle by giving a probability to each element
examples: wchooseCycles(["c c c",5], ["a a a",3], ["f f f",1]).fast(4).note()

// The probability can itself be a pattern
wchooseCycles(["bd(3,8)","<5 0>"], ["hh hh hh",3]).fast(4).s()

name: perlin
description: Generates a continuous pattern of perlin noise, in the range 0..1.

name: berlin
description: Generates a continuous pattern of [berlin noise](conceived by Jame Coyne and Jade Rowland as a joke but turned out to be surprisingly cool and useful, like perlin noise but with sawtooth waves), in the range 0..1.

name: degradeBy
description: Randomly removes events from the pattern by a given amount. 0 = 0% chance of removal 1 = 100% chance of removal

name: degrade
description: Randomly removes 50% of events from the pattern. Shorthand for .degradeBy(0.5)

name: undegradeBy
description: Inverse of degradeBy: Randomly removes events from the pattern by a given amount. 0 = 100% chance of removal 1 = 0% chance of removal Events that would be removed by degradeBy are let through by undegradeBy and vice versa (see second example).

name: undegrade
description: Inverse of degrade: Randomly removes 50% of events from the pattern. Shorthand for .undegradeBy(0.5) Events that would be removed by degrade are let through by undegrade and vice versa (see second example).

name: sometimesBy
description: Randomly applies the given function by the given probability. Similar to someCyclesBy

name: sometimes
description: Applies the given function with a 50% chance

name: someCyclesBy
description: Randomly applies the given function by the given probability on a cycle by cycle basis. Similar to sometimesBy

name: someCycles
description: Shorthand for .someCyclesBy(0.5, fn)

name: often
description: Shorthand for .sometimesBy(0.75, fn)

name: rarely
description: Shorthand for .sometimesBy(0.25, fn)

name: almostNever
description: Shorthand for .sometimesBy(0.1, fn)

name: almostAlways
description: Shorthand for .sometimesBy(0.9, fn)

name: never
description: Shorthand for .sometimesBy(0, fn) (never calls fn)

name: always
description: Shorthand for .sometimesBy(1, fn) (always calls fn)

name: whenKey
description: Do something on a keypress, or array of keypresses Key name reference

name: keyDown
description: returns true when a key or array of keys is held Key name reference

name: cyclesPer
description: A pattern measuring the duration of events, in cycles per event. cyclesPer doesn't have structure itself, but takes structure, and therefore event durations, from the pattern that it is combined with. For example cyclesPer.struct("1 1 [1 1] 1") would give the same as "0.25 0.25 [0.125 0.125] 0.25". See also its reciprocal, per, also known as perCycle.
examples:
sound("bd sd [bd bd] sd*4 [- sd] [bd [bd bd]]")
  .note(cyclesPer.add(20))

name: per
description: A pattern measuring the 'shortness' of events, or in other words, the duration of pattern events, in events per cycle. per doesn't have structure itself, but takes structure, and therefore event durations, from the pattern that it is combined with. For example per.struct("1 1 [1 1] 1") would give the same as "4 4 [8 8] 4". See also its reciprocal, cyclesPer.

name: perx
description: Like per but measures the shortness of events according to an exponential curve. In particular, where the event duration halves, the returned value increases by one. perx.struct("1 1 [1 [1 1]] 1") would therefore be the same as "3 3 [4 [5 5]] 3".

name: csoundm
description:
Sends notes to Csound for rendering with MIDI semantics. The hap value is translated to these Csound pfields:

p1 -- Csound instrument either as a number (1-based, can be a fraction), or as a string name. p2 -- time in beats (usually seconds) from start of performance. p3 -- duration in beats (usually seconds). p4 -- MIDI key number (as a real number, not an integer but in [0, 127]. p5 -- MIDI velocity (as a real number, not an integer but in [0, 127]. p6 -- Strudel controls, as a string.

name: pianoroll
description: Visualises a pattern as a scrolling 'pianoroll', displayed in the background of the editor. To show a pianoroll for all running patterns, use all(pianoroll). To have a pianoroll appear below a pattern instead, prefix with _, e.g.: sound("bd sd")._pianoroll().

params.name: options
params.description: Object containing all the optional following parameters as key value pairs:

params.name: cycles
params.description: number of cycles to be displayed at the same time - defaults to 4

params.name: playhead
params.description: location of the active notes on the time axis - 0 to 1, defaults to 0.5

params.name: labels
params.description: displays labels on individual notes (see the label function) - 0 by default

params.name: flipTime
params.description: reverse the direction of the roll - 0 by default

params.name: flipValues
params.description: reverse the relative location of notes on the value axis - 0 by default

params.name: overscan
params.description: lookup X cycles outside of the cycles window to display notes in advance - 1 by default

params.name: hideNegative
params.description: hide notes with negative time (before starting playing the pattern) - 0 by default

params.name: smear
params.description: notes leave a solid trace - 0 by default

params.name: fold
params.description: notes takes the full value axis width - 0 by default

params.name: active
params.description: hexadecimal or CSS color of the active notes - defaults to #FFCA28

params.name: inactive
params.description: hexadecimal or CSS color of the inactive notes - defaults to #7491D2

params.name: background
params.description: hexadecimal or CSS color of the background - defaults to transparent

params.name: playheadColor
params.description: hexadecimal or CSS color of the line representing the play head - defaults to white

params.name: fill
params.description: notes are filled with color (otherwise only the label is displayed) - 0 by default

params.name: fillActive
params.description: active notes are filled with color - 0 by default

params.name: stroke
params.description: notes are shown with colored borders - 0 by default

params.name: strokeActive
params.description: active notes are shown with colored borders - 0 by default

params.name: hideInactive
params.description: only active notes are shown - 0 by default

params.name: colorizeInactive
params.description: use note color for inactive notes - 1 by default

params.name: fontFamily
params.description: define the font used by notes labels - defaults to 'monospace'

params.name: minMidi
params.description: minimum note value to display on the value axis - defaults to 10

params.name: maxMidi
params.description: maximum note value to display on the value axis - defaults to 90

params.name: autorange
params.description: automatically calculate the minMidi and maxMidi parameters - 0 by default

name: wordfall
description: Displays a vertical pianoroll with event labels. Supports all the same options as pianoroll.

name: pitchwheel
description: Renders a pitch circle to visualize frequencies within one octave

name: spiral
description: Displays a spiral visual.

params.name: options
params.description: Object containing all the optional following parameters as key value pairs:

params.name: stretch
params.description: controls the rotations per cycle ratio, where 1 = 1 cycle / 360 degrees

params.name: cap
params.description: style of line ends: butt (default), round, square

params.name: playheadLength
params.description: length of playhead in rotations, defaults to 0.02

params.name: steady
params.description: steadyness of spiral vs playhead. 1 = spiral doesn't move, playhead does.

params.name: activeColor
params.description: color of active segment. defaults to foreground of theme

params.name: inactiveColor
params.description: color of inactive segments. defaults to gutterForeground of theme

params.name: colorizeInactive
params.description: wether or not to colorize inactive segments, defaults to 0

params.name: fade
params.description: wether or not past and future should fade out. defaults to 1

params.name: logSpiral
params.description: wether or not the spiral should be logarithmic. defaults to 0

name: edoScale
description:
Turns numbers into notes in the given EDO scale (zero indexed).

An EDO scale definition looks like this:

e.g. C:LLsLLLs:2:1 <- this is the C major scale, 12 EDO

e.g. C:LLsLLL:3:1 <- this is the Gorgo 6 note scale, 16 EDO

An EDO scale, e.g. C:LLsLLLs:2:1, consists of a root note (e.g. C) followed by semicolon (':') and then a Large/small step notation sequence (e.g. LLsLLLs) followed by semicolon, then the large step size (e.g. 2) followed by semicolon, then the small step size (e.g. 1).

The number of divisions of the octave is calculated as the sum of the steps in the EDO scale definition.

e.g. C:LLsLLLs:2:1 is 2+2+1+2+2+2+1 = 12 EDO, 7 note scale

e.g. C:LLsLLL:3:1 is 3+3+1+3+3+3 = 16 EDO, 6 note scale

The root note defaults to octave 3, if no octave number is given.

params.name: input
params.description: MIDI device name or index defaulting to 0

name: createCC
description: Implementation for the cc() factory function tied to this specific input.

params.name: chan
params.description: MIDI channel (1-16) or undefined for all channels

name: defaultmidimap
description: configures the default midimap, which is used when no "midimap" port is set

name: midimaps
description: Adds midimaps to the registry. Inside each midimap, control names (e.g. lpf) are mapped to cc numbers.
examples:
midimaps({ mymap: { lpf: 74 } })
$: note("c a f e")
.lpf(sine.slow(4))
.midimap('mymap')
.midi()

name: midi
description: MIDI output: Opens a MIDI output port.

params.name: midiport
params.description: MIDI device name or index defaulting to 0

examples: note("c4").midichan(1).midi('IAC Driver Bus 1', { controller: true, latency: 50 })

name: midin
description:
MIDI input: Opens a MIDI input port to receive MIDI control change messages.

The output is a function that accepts a midi cc value to query as well as (optionally) a midi channel

params.name: input
params.description: MIDI device name or index defaulting to 0

examples:
const cc = await midin('IAC Driver Bus 1')
note("c a f e").lpf(cc(0).range(0, 1000)).lpq(cc(1).range(0, 10)).sound("sawtooth")

const allCC = await midin('IAC Driver Bus 1')
const cc = (ccNum) => allCC(ccNum, 2) // just channel 2
note("c a f e").s("saw")
  .when(cc(0).gt(0), x => x.postgain(0))

name: midikeys
description:
MIDI keyboard: Opens a MIDI input port to receive MIDI keyboard messages.

The note length is fixed as Superdough is not currently set up for undetermined note durations

The 'midichan' control value contains the number of the channel the note is coming from so it could be filtered or manipulated further in the chain.

params.name: input
params.description: MIDI device name or index defaulting to 0

examples:
const kb = await midikeys('Arturia KeyStep 32')
kb("0.5 1")
  .s("saw")
  .add(note(rand.mul(0.3)))
  .lpf(1000).lpe(2).room(0.5)

name: getMidiDeviceNamesString
description: Get a string listing device names for error messages.

name: getDevice
description: Look up a device by index or name. Otherwise return a default device, or fail if none are connected.

name: accelerationX
description: The accelerometer's x-axis value ranges from 0 to 1.

name: accelerationY
description: The accelerometer's y-axis value ranges from 0 to 1.

name: accelerationZ
description: The accelerometer's z-axis value ranges from 0 to 1.

name: gravityX
description: The device's gravity x-axis value ranges from 0 to 1.

name: gravityY
description: The device's gravity y-axis value ranges from 0 to 1.

name: gravityZ

name: rotationAlpha

name: rotationBeta

name: rotationGamma

name: orientationAlpha

name: orientationBeta

name: orientationGamma

name: absoluteOrientationAlpha
description: The device's absolute orientation alpha value ranges from 0 to 1.

name: absoluteOrientationBeta
