name: absoluteOrientationGamma
description: The device's absolute orientation gamma value ranges from 0 to 1.

name: osc
description: Sends each hap as an OSC message, which can be picked up by SuperCollider or any other OSC-enabled software. For more info, read MIDI & OSC in the docs

name: reallocateChannelsIfNeeded
description: Handles dynamic reallocation of input/output channels buffer (channel numbers may vary during lifecycle)

name: readInputs
description: Read next web audio block to input buffers

name: writeOutputs
description: Write next web audio block from output buffers

name: shiftInputBuffers
description: Shift left content of input buffers to receive new web audio block

name: shiftOutputBuffers
description: Shift left content of output buffers to receive new web audio block

name: prepareInputBuffersToSend
description: Copy contents of input buffers to buffer actually sent to process

name: handleOutputBuffersToRetrieve
description: Add contents of output buffers just processed to output buffers

name: generateReverb
description: Generates a reverb impulse response.

params.name: callback
params.description: Function to call when the impulse response has been generated. The impulse response is passed to this function as its parameter. May be called immediately within the current execution context, or later.

name: generateGraph
description: Creates a canvas element showing a graph of the given data.

params.name: data
params.description: An array of numbers, or a Float32Array.

params.name: min
params.description: Minimum value of data for the graph (lower edge).

params.name: max
params.description: Maximum value of data in the graph (upper edge).

name: applyGradualLowpass
description: Applies a constantly changing lowpass filter to the given sound.

params.name: callback
params.description: May be called immediately within the current execution context, or later.

name: getDur
description:
Returns the duration, in seconds, of the given sample. Has optional param n (for instance, the 2 in s("casio:2"))

Note: must be called with await, otherwise you'll get a pending Promise object.

name: samples
description: Loads a collection of samples to use with s

name: setMaxPolyphony
description: Set the max polyphony. If notes are ringing out via release then they will start to die out in first-in-first-out order once the max polyphony has been hit

name: setGainCurve
description: Apply a function to all gains provided in patterns. Can be used to rescale gain to be quadratic, exponential, etc. rather than linear
examples:
setGainCurve((x) => x * x) // quadratic gain
s("bd*4").gain(0.5) // equivalent to 0.25 gain normally

name: aliasBank
description: Register an alias for a bank of sounds. Optionally accepts a single argument map of bank aliases. Optionally accepts a single argument string of a path to a JSON file containing bank aliases.

params.name: alias
params.description: The alias to use for the bank

name: soundAlias
description: Register an alias for a sound.

params.name: alias
params.description: The alias to use for the sound

name: pickAndRename
description: Selects entries from source and renames them via map

name: tables
description: Loads a collection of wavetables to use with s

name: applyHannWindow
description: Apply Hann window in-place

name: computeMagnitudes
description: Compute squared magnitudes for peak finding

name: findPeaks
description: Find peaks in spectrum magnitudes

name: shiftPeaks
description: Shift peaks and regions of influence by pitchFactor into new specturm

name: chyx
description: BYTE BEATS

name: crossfade
description: Equal Power Crossfade function. Smoothly transitions between signals A and B, maintaining consistent perceived loudness.

params.name: a
params.description: Signal A (can be a single value or an array value in buffer processing).

params.name: b
params.description: Signal B (can be a single value or an array value in buffer processing).

params.name: m
params.description: Crossfade parameter (0.0 = all A, 1.0 = all B, 0.5 = equal mix).

name: transpose
description:
Change the pitch of each value by the given amount. Expects numbers or note strings as values. The amount can be given as a number of semitones or as a string in interval short notation. If you don't care about enharmonic correctness, just use numbers. Otherwise, pass the interval of the form: ST where S is the degree number and T the type of interval with

  • M = major
  • m = minor
  • P = perfect
  • A = augmented
  • d = diminished

Examples intervals:

  • 1P = unison
  • 3M = major third
  • 3m = minor third
  • 4P = perfect fourth
  • 4A = augmented fourth
  • 5P = perfect fifth
  • 5d = diminished fifth

params.name: amount
params.description: Either number of semitones or interval string.

name: scaleTranspose
description: Transposes notes inside the scale by the number of steps. Expected to be called on a Pattern which already has a {@link Pattern#scale}

name: scale
description:
Turns numbers into notes in the scale (zero indexed) or quantizes notes to a scale.

When describing notes via numbers, note that negative numbers can be used to wrap backwards in the scale as well as sharps or flats to produce notes outside of the scale.

Also sets scale for other scale operations, like {@link Pattern#scaleTranspose}.

A scale consists of a root note (e.g. c4, c, f#, bb4) followed by semicolon (':') and then a scale type.

The scale name must be written without spaces (because it would be interpreted as a multi-step pattern otherwise). If your scale name includes spaces, replace them with colons.

The root note defaults to octave 3, if no octave number is given.

name: addVoicings
description: Adds a new custom voicing dictionary.

name: voicings
description: DEPRECATED: still works, but it is recommended you use .voicing instead (without s). Turns chord symbols into voicings, using the smoothest voice leading possible. Uses chord-voicings package.
examples: stack("<C^7 A7 Dm7 G7>".voicings('lefthand'), "<C3 A2 D3 G2>").note()

name: rootNotes
description: Maps the chords of the incoming pattern to root notes in the given octave.

name: voicing
description:
Turns chord symbols into voicings. You can use the following control params:

  • chord: Note, followed by chord symbol, e.g. C Am G7 Bb^7
  • dict: voicing dictionary to use, falls back to default dictionary
  • anchor: the note that is used to align the chord
  • mode: how the voicing is aligned to the anchor
    • below: top note <= anchor
    • duck: top note <= anchor, anchor excluded
    • above: bottom note >= anchor
  • offset: whole number that shifts the voicing up or down to the next voicing
  • n: if set, the voicing is played like a scale. Overshooting numbers will be octaved

All of the above controls are optional, except chord. If you pass a pattern of strings to voicing, they will be interpreted as chords.

name: fscope
description: Renders an oscilloscope for the frequency domain of the audio signal.

params.name: color
params.description: line color as hex or color name. defaults to white.

params.name: pos
params.description: y-position relative to screen height. 0 = top, 1 = bottom of screen

name: scope
description: Renders an oscilloscope for the time domain of the audio signal.

params.name: align
params.description: if 1, the scope will be aligned to the first zero crossing. defaults to 1

params.name: color
params.description: line color as hex or color name. defaults to white.

params.name: pos
params.description: y-position relative to screen height. 0 = top, 1 = bottom of screen

params.name: trigger
params.description: amplitude value that is used to align the scope. defaults to 0.

name: spectrum
description: Renders a spectrum analyzer for the incoming audio signal.

name: tune
description: Assumes pattern contains numerical scale degrees on the i control (see examples below). Accepts a scale name or list of frequencies (see all available names at the link on the reference). Returns a new pattern with all values mapped to a frequency ratio. Similar to xen.

name: xen
description: Assumes a numerical pattern of scale steps, and a scale. Scales accepted are all preset scale names of tune, arbitrary edos such as 31edo, or an array of frequency ratios. Assumes scales repeat at octave (2/1). Returns a new pattern with all values mapped to their associated frequency, assuming a base frequency of 220hz.

name: withBase
description: Assumes pattern of frequencies tuned to some base frequency, such as the output of xen Because xen defaults to 220Hz, so will withBase. but you can specify a different original base with the standard optional array syntax ':'

name: ftranspose
description:
Frequency transpose. Assumes pattern either has freq set, or has values that can be interpreted as frequencies amt has optional edoSize param, defaults to 12. If haps have edoSize param set, such as from the output of xen("31edo"), ftrans will fallback to that instead of 12 as the default.

Transposes the frequency by amt edoSteps
examples:
// to transpose by steps of an edo, use "step:edo" :
i("0 7 8 18").xen("31edo").ftrans("<0 1:31 1:12>")

// it can also work with frequency values directly
freq("200 300 400").ftrans("<0 7:31 7>")
